purpose: Craft examples for modern mystery, abnormal readings, rule spaces, hotel/restaurant cases, investigators, and evidence-driven suspense.
source_priority_rules: Evidence, devices, reports, locations, and suspect roles must come from source or locked payload.
Suspense should arise from contradiction, abnormal measurement, blocked exit, identity mismatch, or time pressure.
Do not add weapons, crimes, police authority, or surveillance tech unless source supports them.
genre_boundary: Track evidence chain, who knows what, who can see what, and what changed on screen.
Make the abnormal visible through readings, behavior, space, sound, or object state.
End beats with a new question, contradiction, or choice rather than generic fear.

scene_card_examples.visual: The investigator checks a locked device or source-grounded clue; the reading contradicts the visible safe space.
scene_card_examples.voiceover: The data says danger, but the room is pretending nothing happened.

asset_goal.purpose: device, reading, investigator reaction, location anchor

scene_card_examples.visual: A medium shot keeps the protagonist, exit, and evidence object in one frame so the audience sees the spatial problem.
scene_card_examples.voiceover: The way out is visible; the problem is that the evidence says it is not open.

script_scene_examples.visual: The investigator repeats a measurement, then turns the device toward the locked location rather than accusing a person too early.
script_scene_examples.voiceover: The first suspect is not a person; it is the room itself.

dialogue.speaker: investigator
dialogue.line: Same reading. The problem is inside this room.

script_scene_examples.action_result: The evidence redirects attention from character suspicion to spatial anomaly.

dialogue_style_examples: Investigators should speak in evidence, readings, time, location, or contradiction.
Protagonists should ask actionable questions, not narrate fear.
Do not reveal answers before the evidence chain earns them.

negative_examples.bad: A detective instantly explains the whole mystery.
negative_examples.why_bad: It removes suspense and skips evidence progression.

negative_examples.bad: A new hidden camera solves the scene without source setup.
negative_examples.why_bad: Unsupported technology creates a false solution.

forbidden_drift: Do not add unsupported law-enforcement roles, weapons, CCTV, secret rooms, or crimes.
Do not let characters know information they did not observe or receive.
Do not solve a contradiction in the same beat that introduces it unless source does.

image: Lock device screen, evidence object, exit, and room layout.
video: Use insert shots of readings, doors, reflections, and hands as continuity bridges when source-grounded.
